rationale: The cited paper is used in the theoretical background to support a key mechanism: diminishing sensitivity in subjective judgments as objective magnitude increases. The citing authors explicitly invoke Chandon and Wansink (2007) as a marketing-domain demonstration of this psychophysical pattern, then use that logic to justify their concavity prediction for assortment benefits. Although the citation is not tied to method or data reuse, it plays a meaningful conceptual role in framing the paper's core argument.
relation_description: The citing paper applies the psychophysical diminishing-sensitivity idea evidenced by Chandon and Wansink (2007) to a different context, arguing that the marginal advantage of larger assortments shrinks as option attractiveness rises. It uses the cited work as conceptual support for this concave value mechanism.
